Journal.Title: American journal of physiology. Cell physiology

Article.ArticleTitle: Skeletal muscle hypertrophy: cell growth is cell growth.

Abstract.AbstractText: Roberts et al. have provided an insightful counterpoint to our review article on the utility of the synergist ablation model. The purpose of this review is to provide some further dialogue regarding the strengths and weaknesses of the synergist ablation model. Specifically, we highlight that the robustness of the model overshadows surgical limitations. We also compare the transcriptomic responses to synergist ablation in mice and resistance exercise in humans to identify common pathways. We conclude that "cell growth is cell growth" and that the mechanisms available to cells to accumulate biomass and increase in size are similar across cell types and independent of the rate of growth.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: No conflicts of interest, financial or otherwise, are declared by the authors.
